DP.048.0000:
Ah!
That’s Oreburgh’s Gym Badge!\f
I see, I see!
You’ve defeated my son.\f
But that’s no surprise.
He still has much to learn.\f
In place of my son Roark, I, Byron,
will take your challenge!\f
DP.048.0001:
You were strong enough to take down
my prized team of Pokémon.\f
In recognition of that power,
I give you this: the Mine Badge!\f
DP.048.0002:
[0103,0000] received
the Mine Badge from Byron!
DP.048.0003:
Having the Mine Badge enables you to
use the hidden move Strength anytime\r
outside of battle.\f
And you now have six Gym Badges.\f
That means all Pokémon up to Lv. 70
will obey you without question.\f
Here!
Take this, too!\f
DP.048.0004:
That [0108,0000] contains the move
[0106,0001].\f
If it hits, it may also lower the
target’s Special Defense.
DP.048.0005:
Gwahahahaha!
The Sinnoh region is vast!\f
Tough Trainers like you and
[0103,0001] crop up constantly\r
to challenge the old guard!\f
With more young Trainers like my son
and you two taking charge, the\r
future of Pokémon is bright!\f
But I’m not stepping aside just yet!\f
I merely need to rededicate myself
to training on Iron Island.
DP.048.0006:
Hey, there!
Champ-to-be!\f
The Gym Leader here uses Steel-type
Pokémon.\f
Steel-type Pokémon have high Defense,
so you’d have to hit them hard!
DP.048.0007:
Oh! [0103,0000]!
You’ve beaten the Gym Leader!\f
Good going!
That makes it six Gym Badges!\f
I tell you what, you’re really turning
into one fine Trainer.\f
If you were to say you’re going to
try to become the Pokémon Champ...\f
No one’d laugh at you now!
I’m in your corner, kiddo!
DP.048.0008:
Canalave City Pokémon Gym
Leader: Byron\f
Certified Trainers:
[0103,0001]
